summary.intent: Address a second round of Codex review findings on the image_cropper component, fixing 6 robustness/correctness issues identified after the first pass.
summary.outcome: All 6 second-pass findings resolved with 8 new TDD tests (all passing), spec synced, snapshots updated, clippy clean, and xci-fast green. Changes committed and pushed, ready to reply/resolve Codex threads and re-trigger.

repo: The project uses a TDD workflow: write failing tests first, then implement fixes, confirmed by 'Confirm round-2 tests fail' step before any implementation.
Codex is used as an automated code review tool that runs in passes; findings from a second pass can reveal deeper consequences of first-pass changes.
The project uses cargo xci-fast as a pre-push gate combining tests, clippy, and snapshot validation.
Snapshots must be explicitly re-accepted when output changes (e.g., adding alt attribute to img elements).
The Bindable abstraction has both an inert internal slot and a controlled slot — constraining only the internal is insufficient when the value is controlled; both slots must be updated.

code.finding: normalize_rotation had an uncovered non-finite branch that needed a test feeding NaN rotation through SetCropArea to exercise it.
f64::clamp panics when min > max or bounds are NaN; replaced with a custom clamp_zoom using .max().min() chaining which is NaN-tolerant and order-safe.

zoom and flip props must be treated as initial-only (uncontrolled, like defaultValue) — SyncProps should re-clamp current zoom into new bounds rather than overwriting the user's value on unrelated prop changes.
AspectRatio::Fixed must reject non-finite, non-positive, and zero values via as_ratio returning None (falling back to unconstrained) rather than propagating invalid ratios.
New sanitize_crop helper enforces: finite values, floor clamping, in-bounds geometry, and normalized rotation — applied at SetCropArea, init, SyncCrop, and Reset.
Decorative img elements must include alt="" for accessibility; image_attrs was missing this attribute.

workflow: Multi-pass AI review (Codex) is effective at finding deeper issues: first pass resolves surface bugs, second pass reveals consequences and edge cases introduced by the fixes.
When fixing a controlled component pattern, always trace the full write path — both the internal representation and the controlled slot must reflect constraints.
Clippy nits should be resolved before committing; two appeared after the implementation pass and were caught by running clippy explicitly.
Coverage gaps should be checked after implementing fixes, not just after writing tests — new code paths (helpers, guard branches) need explicit test coverage.

friction: First-pass SyncCrop fix was incomplete — the reasoning about 'inert internal' vs controlled slot was wrong, requiring a second fix in round 2.
Multiple Read tool calls were needed to locate specific sections before editing, suggesting the file is large and navigation is non-trivial.
Snapshots had to be re-accepted after adding alt attribute, requiring an extra acceptance step that broke the initial test run.
open_items: Reply and resolve the 6 second-pass Codex review threads on the PR.
Re-trigger Codex for a potential third pass to verify all findings are addressed.
The unreachable resize guard branch noted during coverage analysis was not covered and marked as intentional — may warrant a comment or explicit #[allow(unreachable_code)] annotation.
